draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers organic, approachable UI elements over rigid geometry
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Explore warmer, more human-centered interface aesthetics with smooth transitions and gentle shapes
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor clarity and precision through defined edges; test minimalist or geometric design languages

facade.hypothesis: An AI workspace benefits from persistent left navigation paired with card-based content areas rather than tabbed interfaces
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore denser sidebar patterns, multi-column card layouts, and persistent context
facade.rejectImplies: Test tab-based navigation, full-width layouts, and modal-driven interactions

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers organic, approachable UI elements over rigid geometry
evidence.implication: Explore warmer, more human-centered interface aesthetics with smooth transitions and gentle shapes

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users may prefer either pre-built data summaries (dashboard) or flexible blank canvas (notebook) as their primary entry point
facade.axisTargeted: Structure vs. Freeform — whether the interface should guide with pre-composed layouts or trust users to build their own
facade.label: Dashboard or notebook
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward guided structure, predefined layouts, and curated starting states for efficiency
facade.rejectImplies: User leans toward blank canvas, minimal scaffolding, and full control over information organization from scratch

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate, click-based interactions without animated transitions in an AI workspace where speed and directness matter more than fluidity.
facade.label: Instant snap
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize instantaneous state changes, direct manipulation, and minimal motion—crisp, responsive clicks over smoothed animations.
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should embrace animated transitions, eased movements, and gesture-based fluidity—motion should guide the user's eye and create a sense of continuity between states.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer AI workspace guidance that feels like talking to a colleague rather than reading documentation
facade.label: Casual, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design voice as approachable and chat-like; use contractions; reduce formality in all copy
facade.rejectImplies: Maintain formal, authoritative voice; prioritize clarity and precision over warmth; keep professional distance

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer minimal color schemes that reduce cognitive load in an AI workspace, allowing content and data to take focus
facade.axisTargeted: Color saturation and complexity (light vs. dark spectrum expansion)
facade.label: Monochrome palette
facade.acceptImplies: Explore desaturated, single-hue or grayscale-forward interfaces with accent colors used sparingly for hierarchy and alerts
facade.rejectImplies: User likely wants color as a primary design language; probe toward multi-hue, vibrant, or chromatic-rich interfaces next

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may gravitate toward either sterile-technical (cool, bright, minimal) or human-centered (warm, soft, inviting) atmospheres. This probe tests whether the builder wants their workspace to feel approachable and organic or clinical and precise.
facade.label: Warm lighting
facade.acceptImplies: Builder leans toward human-centered, inviting aesthetic—likely values comfort, accessibility, and softer visual language in their AI workspace.
facade.rejectImplies: Builder leans toward cool, technical, precise aesthetic—likely values clarity, efficiency, and cleaner (possibly brighter or more minimal) visual language.

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
